name_en: Starting Point of the Opening of the Hokkaido Railway
description_en: This is a monument (historic site) marking the starting point of the opening of the Hokkaido railway. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Otaru City, designated on 1966-11-03.

name_romaji: Kamikawa-gun Nōsaku Shikenjo Jimushotō (Chūbetsuputo Ekiteidai-ichi Bieisha)
name_en: Office Building of the Kamikawa County Agricultural Experiment Station (First Chubetsuputo Post Station Bieisha)
description_en: This is a tangible cultural property (structure) consisting of an office building of the Kamikawa County agricultural experiment station. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Asahikawa City, designated on 1966-01-15, located at Kamui 1-jo 1-chome.

name_en: Kunimi Monument on Mount Chikabumi
description_en: This is a monument (historic site) known as the Kunimi stone monument on Mount Chikabumi. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Asahikawa City, designated on 1966-01-15, located at Arashiyama, Etanbetsu-cho.

name_en: Plum Tree
description_en: This is a monument (natural monument) consisting of a plum tree. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Asahikawa City, designated on 1966-01-15, located in the schoolyard of Asahikawa Daiichi Elementary School, Yonehara, Higashiasahikawa-cho.

name_en: Kamuikotan Pothole Cluster
description_en: This is a monument (natural monument) consisting of a cluster of potholes at Kamuikotan. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Asahikawa City, designated on 1966-07-25, located on the Ishikari River bed at Kamuikotan, Kamui-cho.

name_en: Nagayama Tonden Colonist Soldier's House
description_en: This is a tangible cultural property (structure) consisting of a tonden colonist soldier's house at Nagayama. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Asahikawa City, designated on 1970-07-06, located at the Asahikawa City Museum, Kagura 3-jo 7-chome.

name_en: Sericulture Farmhouse
description_en: This is a tangible cultural property (structure) consisting of a sericulture (silkworm-raising) farmhouse. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Asahikawa City, designated on 1973-03-19, located at Mizuho 1576-1, Higashiasahikawa-cho.

name_en: Company Records of the Asahikawa Military Colony (Higashiasahikawa Tonden)
description_en: This is a tangible cultural property (historical document) consisting of the company records of the Asahikawa military colony (Higashiasahikawa tonden). It is a municipally designated cultural property of Asahikawa City, designated on 1981-02-20, located at the Asahikawa Heison Memorial Hall, Higashiasahikawa Minami 1-jo 6-chome.

name_en: Company Records of the Asahikawa Military Colony (Higashiasahikawa Tonden), Additional Portion
description_en: This is a tangible cultural property (historical document) consisting of an additional portion of the company records of the Asahikawa military colony (Higashiasahikawa tonden). It is a municipally designated cultural property of Asahikawa City, designated on 2006-03-30, located at the Asahikawa Heison Memorial Hall, Higashiasahikawa Minami 1-jo 6-chome.

name_romaji: Nishikimachi Go Iseki Shutsudo no Onoe
name_en: Axe Handle Excavated from the Nishikimachi No. 5 Site
description_en: This is a tangible cultural property (archaeological material) consisting of an axe handle excavated from the Nishikimachi No. 5 site. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Asahikawa City, designated on 1986-08-20, located at the Asahikawa City Museum, Kagura 3-jo 7-chome.

name_en: Warabite Sword
description_en: This is a tangible cultural property (archaeological material) consisting of a warabite (bracken-hilted) sword. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Asahikawa City, designated on 1986-08-20, located at the Asahikawa City Museum, Kagura 3-jo 7-chome.

name_en: Former Kamuikotan Station Building
description_en: This is a tangible cultural property (structure) consisting of the former Kamuikotan station building. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Asahikawa City, designated on 1991-03-15, located at Kasuga 197-1, Etanbetsu-cho.

name_en: Records Relating to the 7th Division
description_en: This is a tangible cultural property (historical document) consisting of records relating to the 7th Division. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Asahikawa City, designated on 2006-03-30, located at the Hokuchin Memorial Hall, JGSDF 2nd Division Asahikawa Garrison, Shunko-cho.

name_en: Chiri Yukie's Posthumous Manuscript Notebook
description_en: This is a tangible cultural property (historical document) consisting of the posthumous manuscript notebook of Chiri Yukie. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Asahikawa City, designated on 2006-03-30, located at the Asahikawa City Museum, Kagura 3-jo 7-chome.

name_en: Kannon Statue Made by Enku
description_en: This is a tangible cultural property consisting of a Kannon (Avalokitesvara) statue made by Enku. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Noboribetsu City, designated on 1993-09-02.

name_en: Sho-Kannon Statue Made by Enku
description_en: This is a tangible cultural property consisting of a Sho-Kannon (Arya Avalokitesvara) statue made by Enku. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Noboribetsu City, designated on 1993-09-02.

name_en: Kannon Statue Made by Takamura Toun
description_en: This is a tangible cultural property consisting of a Kannon (Avalokitesvara) statue made by Takamura Toun. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Noboribetsu City, designated on 1993-09-02.

name_romaji: Hino Aiki no "Meiji Ninen Ikō Katakurake Hokkaidō Ijū Tenmatsu"
name_en: Hino Aiki's "Account of the Katakura Family's Migration to Hokkaido from 1869 Onward"
description_en: This is a tangible cultural property consisting of Hino Aiki's account of the Katakura family's migration to Hokkaido from 1869 onward. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Noboribetsu City, designated on 1993-09-02.

name_en: Horobetsu Mine Lion Dance
description_en: This is a folk cultural property consisting of the lion dance (shishimai) of the Horobetsu mine. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Noboribetsu City, designated on 1993-09-02.

name_en: Kurosawa Family Historical Materials
description_en: This is a tangible cultural property consisting of historical materials of the Kurosawa family. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Noboribetsu City, designated on 1998-02-10.

name_en: Carbonized Wood of the Noboribetsu Fossil Forest (Sakhalin Fir)
description_en: This is a monument (historic site, scenic beauty, natural monument) consisting of carbonized wood (Sakhalin fir) from the Noboribetsu fossil forest. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Noboribetsu City, designated on 2017-02-01.

name_en: Horobetsu Village Office Documents
description_en: This is a tangible cultural property consisting of documents of the Horobetsu village office. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Noboribetsu City, designated on 2019-04-26.

name_en: Katakura Family and Retainers Pioneer Commemoration Monument
description_en: This is a monument (historic site) consisting of a pioneer commemoration monument for the Katakura family and their retainers. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Noboribetsu City, designated on 2022-03-30.

name_en: Record of Joso
description_en: This is a tangible cultural property consisting of the Record of Joso. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Noboribetsu City, designated on 2023-02-24.

name_en: Ishikawa Shuji Collection
description_en: This is a tangible cultural property consisting of the collection of Ishikawa Shuji. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Noboribetsu City, designated on 2025-06-04.

name_en: One Seated Statue of Kannon Bodhisattva Made by Enku
description_en: This is a tangible cultural property consisting of one seated statue of Kannon (Avalokitesvara) Bodhisattva made by Enku. It is a town-designated cultural property of Oshamambe Town, designated on 2011-03-23.

name_romaji: Ainu Minzoku Ishō "Ruunpe" Nichaku, "Chijiri" Nichaku / Kei Yonchaku
name_en: Ainu Traditional Garments: Two Ruunpe and Two Chijiri (Four in Total)
description_en: This is a folk cultural property (tangible) consisting of Ainu traditional garments: two ruunpe and two chijiri, four in total. It is a town-designated cultural property of Oshamambe Town, designated on 2011-03-23.

name_en: Dugout Canoe
description_en: This is a folk cultural property (tangible) consisting of a dugout canoe. It is a town-designated cultural property of Oshamambe Town, designated on 2011-03-23.

name_en: Former Powder Magazine of the Wanishi Tonden Colonist Soldiers
description_en: This is a tangible cultural property consisting of the former powder magazine of the Wanishi tonden colonist soldiers. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Muroran City, designated on 1972-03-23, located within the precincts of Nakashima Shrine, Miyanomori-cho.

name_en: Wanishi Tonden Colonist Soldiers Memorial Monument
description_en: This is a tangible cultural property consisting of a memorial monument to the Wanishi tonden colonist soldiers. It is a municipally designated cultural property of Muroran City, designated on 1972-03-23, located within the precincts of Nakashima Shrine, Miyanomori-cho.

name_en: Site of the Mashike Civil Engineering Branch Office
description_en: This is a monument (historic site) marking the site of the Mashike civil engineering branch office. It is a town-designated cultural property of Mashike Town, designated on 1979-07-25.

name_romaji: Kanritsu Sapporo Byōin Mashike Shutchōjo Ato
name_en: Site of the Mashike Branch of the Government Sapporo Hospital
description_en: This is a monument (historic site) marking the site of the Mashike branch of the government-run Sapporo Hospital. It is a town-designated cultural property of Mashike Town, designated on 1978-06-30.

name_en: Site of the Mashike Trading Post
description_en: This is a monument (historic site) marking the site of the Mashike unjoya (trading post). It is a town-designated cultural property of Mashike Town, designated on 1978-06-30.

name_en: Encampment Site of the Mito Domain Lord's Party
description_en: This is a monument (historic site) marking the encampment site of the party of the Mito domain lord. It is a town-designated cultural property of Mashike Town, designated on 1980-07-01.

name_en: Site of the Colonization Commission Mashike Ship Inspection Office
description_en: This is a monument (historic site) marking the site of the Mashike ship inspection office of the Colonization Commission (Kaitakushi). It is a town-designated cultural property of Mashike Town, designated on 1978-06-30.

name_en: Site of the Irifune-cho Beach Teahouse
description_en: This is a monument (historic site) marking the site of the Irifune-cho beach teahouse. It is a town-designated cultural property of Mashike Town, designated on 1979-07-25.

name_en: Second Battery of the Akita Domain Main Encampment
description_en: This is a monument (historic site) consisting of the second battery of the Akita domain main encampment. It is a town-designated cultural property of Mashike Town, designated on 1980-07-01.

name_romaji: Ringo Saibai no Chichi Fujiwara Fudekichi Ō no Hi
name_en: Monument to Fujiwara Fudekichi, Father of Apple Cultivation
description_en: This is a monument (historic site) commemorating Fujiwara Fudekichi, called the father of apple cultivation. It is a town-designated cultural property of Mashike Town, designated on 1983-07-21.

name_en: Site of the Teshio Province Fisheries Association Salmon and Trout Hatchery
description_en: This is a monument (historic site) marking the site of the salmon and trout hatchery of the Teshio Province Fisheries Association. It is a town-designated cultural property of Mashike Town, designated on 1999-02-10.

name_en: Ofuyu Cold Spring
description_en: This is a monument (historic site) consisting of the Ofuyu cold spring water. It is a town-designated cultural property of Mashike Town, designated on 1979-07-25.
